papers.title: Properties of Fe–Mn–Al alloys with different Mn contents using density functional theory
papers.paper_scope: First-principles DFT investigation of chemical stability, electronic structures, mechanical properties, and Debye temperature of Fe1-xMnxAl alloys to obtain complete data for design of high-temperature structural and functional materials.

alloys.alloys_notes: Pure Fe reference calculated for cohesion and formation enthalpy baseline.
Pure Mn reference calculated for cohesion and formation enthalpy baseline.
Pure Al reference calculated for cohesion and formation enthalpy baseline.

processes.description: First-principles DFT calculations using CASTEP code with GGA-PBE, ultrasoft pseudopotentials, plane-wave cutoff 450 eV, Monkhorst-Pack 8x8x8 k-point mesh; geometry optimization of Fe1-xMnxAl cubic supercells.

computational_details.simulation_method: First-principles DFT calculations implemented in CASTEP
computational_details.potential_model: Ultrasoft pseudopotentials with GGA-PBE exchange-correlation functional
computational_details.validation_against_experiment: Calculated lattice parameters and properties compared favorably with available experimental and theoretical data (see Table 1 and text).

analytical_model.model_note: Used to evaluate thermodynamic stability of Fe1-xMnxAl alloys from total energies and bulk reference energies.
Used to evaluate thermodynamic stability from total energies and isolated atom energies.

analytical_model.model_name: Voigt-Reuss-Hill (VRH) approximation for polycrystalline elastic moduli
analytical_model.model_note: Combines Voigt and Reuss averages to calculate bulk modulus B, shear modulus G, Young's modulus E, and Poisson's ratio from single-crystal elastic constants C11, C12, C44.
Hv = 2(k^2 G)^{0.585} - 3, with k = G/B, giving hardness from shear modulus and Pugh ratio.
Includes universal anisotropic index A^U, anisotropy of bulk modulus A_B, shear modulus A_G, and shear anisotropic factors A1, A2, A3 for (100), (010), (001) planes.

analytical_model.model_name: Debye temperature and sound velocity calculation
analytical_model.model_note: Calculates Debye temperature and longitudinal, transverse, and average sound velocities from elastic moduli and mass density.

unmapped_findings: Fe–Mn–Al alloys exhibit a combination of covalent and metallic bonding, which results in good electronic conductivity, relatively high melting points and high hardness.
FeAl is the most stable alloy in the Fe1-xMnxAl system based on formation enthalpy and cohesive energy.
MnAl exhibits the largest elastic anisotropy among Fe–Mn–Al alloys (A^U = 3.80), while FeAl has the smallest (A^U = 0.56).
Among mixed Fe-Mn-Al compounds, Fe0.5Mn0.5Al has the largest elastic anisotropy (A^U = 2.06).
The Debye temperature decreases from FeAl to Fe0.5Mn0.5Al with increasing Mn content, then increases to Fe0.25Mn0.75Al before decreasing again to MnAl.
The change in hardness and Debye temperature follows a similar trend, likely due to bonding features.
FeAl and Fe0.875Mn0.125Al are brittle (B/G < 1.75), while all other Fe–Mn–Al alloys studied are ductile.
The Fe–Mn–Al system exhibits potential as high-temperature structural materials, high-strength materials, and functional materials according to the introduction and conclusions.

Fig. 9 shows planar projections of Young's modulus on (001) and (110) planes, indicating strong directional dependence and anisotropy; pure-text pipeline cannot read exact angular values from the polar plots.
